title: Speaking: Shopping and Asking Prices
description: Practice asking for prices and stating what item you want in a store.

blocks.title: Scene 1: Buying a Souvenir
blocks.context: You see a beautiful red fan (sen-su), but you don't know the price. You want to ask the clerk and buy it.
blocks.goal: Ask how much the fan is using 'kore', and decide to buy it.
usefulPhrases: これは いくらですか？(Kore wa ikura desu ka?) - How much is this?
ひゃくえんです。(Hyaku en desu.) - It is 100 yen. (Shopkeeper)
じゃあ、これを ください。(Jaa, kore wo kudasai.) - Well then, I will take this.

blocks.title: Scene 2: Specifying an Item
blocks.context: The pens are behind the counter. You want to point out the blue one.
blocks.goal: Use 'sore' (that near the listener) or 'are' (that over there) and colors.
usefulPhrases: あれは なんですか？(Are wa nan desu ka?) - What is that over there?
それは あおい ペンです。(Sore wa aoi pen desu.) - That is a blue pen.
あおい ペンを ください。(Aoi pen wo kudasai.) - A blue pen, please.

blocks.prompt: Practice saying numbers out loud! Count from 1 to 10 in Japanese, then from 10, 20, 30... up to 100. Record yourself or practice with a mirror.
hints: 1-10: ichi, ni, san, yon, go, roku, nana, hachi, kyuu, juu
10-100: juu, ni-juu, san-juu, yon-juu, go-juu, roku-juu, nana-juu, hachi-juu, kyuu-juu, hyaku
Make sure your vowels are clear. Keep 'juu' a long 'u' sound.
